metadata.description: PNLE Question Bank - Add your questions here following the same format

questions.subject: Fundamentals of Nursing
questions.question: A nurse is assessing a client's radial pulse. Which finding should be reported to the physician immediately?
choices: A. A rate of 72 beats per minute
B. A rate of 110 beats per minute with irregular rhythm
C. A rate of 60 beats per minute with regular rhythm
D. A rate of 80 beats per minute with strong amplitude
questions.rationale: A pulse rate of 110 bpm with irregular rhythm (tachycardia with dysrhythmia) is an abnormal finding that should be reported immediately. Normal pulse rate is 60-100 bpm with regular rhythm. An irregular rhythm may indicate cardiac arrhythmia.

questions.subject: Fundamentals of Nursing
questions.question: A nurse is caring for a client diagnosed with active pulmonary tuberculosis. Which type of isolation precautions should the nurse implement?
choices: A. Contact precautions
B. Droplet precautions
C. Airborne precautions
D. Standard precautions only
questions.rationale: Pulmonary tuberculosis is spread via airborne transmission (droplet nuclei <5 microns that remain suspended in air). Airborne precautions include a negative pressure room, N95 respirator for healthcare workers, and surgical mask on patient during transport.

questions.subject: Fundamentals of Nursing
questions.question: When preparing to administer medications, which action by the nurse best demonstrates safe medication administration?
choices: A. Preparing medications for all assigned clients at the same time
B. Verifying the 10 rights of medication administration
C. Crushing all medications before administration
D. Documenting medication administration before giving the drug
questions.rationale: The nurse should always verify the rights of medication administration: right patient, right medication, right dose, right route, right time, right documentation, right reason, right response, right education, and right to refuse. This ensures medication safety.

questions.subject: Medical-Surgical Nursing
questions.question: A client is admitted with chest pain. The ECG shows ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF. Which area of the heart is most likely affected?
choices: A. Anterior wall
B. Lateral wall
C. Inferior wall
D. Posterior wall
questions.rationale: ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF indicates inferior wall MI. These leads view the inferior surface of the heart supplied by the right coronary artery (RCA). Anterior MI shows changes in V1-V4; lateral MI in I, aVL, V5-V6.

questions.subject: Medical-Surgical Nursing
questions.question: A nurse is caring for a client with COPD who is receiving oxygen therapy. What oxygen flow rate is most appropriate for this client?
choices: A. 6-8 L/min via simple face mask
B. 1-3 L/min via nasal cannula
C. 10-15 L/min via non-rebreather mask
D. 4-6 L/min via nasal cannula
questions.rationale: COPD clients rely on hypoxic drive for breathing stimulation. High oxygen levels can suppress their respiratory drive. Low-flow oxygen (1-3 L/min via nasal cannula) maintains SpO2 at 88-92%, preventing oxygen-induced hypoventilation while treating hypoxemia.

questions.subject: Medical-Surgical Nursing
questions.question: A client with Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is found unresponsive with a blood glucose of 40 mg/dL. Which intervention is the priority?
choices: A. Administer insulin per sliding scale
B. Give 15g of fast-acting carbohydrate orally
C. Administer 50% dextrose (D50W) intravenously
D. Call the physician before doing anything
questions.rationale: The client is unresponsive (altered mental status), making oral administration contraindicated due to aspiration risk. D50W IV is the priority treatment for severe hypoglycemia with unconsciousness. Never give oral glucose to an unconscious patient.

questions.subject: Maternal & Child Health Nursing
questions.question: A pregnant client at 28 weeks gestation reports decreased fetal movement. Which nursing action is most appropriate initially?
choices: A. Reassure the client that decreased movement is normal
B. Instruct the client to perform kick counts
C. Immediately schedule a cesarean section
D. Administer oxytocin to stimulate fetal activity
questions.rationale: Kick counts (fetal movement counting) is the first intervention for decreased fetal movement. The client should count 10 movements within 2 hours. If fewer than 10 movements occur, further assessment (NST, BPP) is warranted. Decreased fetal movement can indicate fetal compromise.

questions.subject: Maternal & Child Health Nursing
questions.question: During a vaginal examination, the nurse discovers a prolapsed umbilical cord. What is the priority nursing action?
choices: A. Place the client in Trendelenburg position and call for help
B. Push the cord back into the uterus
C. Prepare the client for immediate cesarean section
D. Apply continuous external fetal monitoring
questions.rationale: Priority is relieving cord compression. The nurse should place a gloved hand in the vagina to lift the presenting part off the cord, while simultaneously positioning the client in knee-chest or Trendelenburg position to use gravity. Call for help immediately. Never push the cord back as this can cause vasospasm.

questions.subject: Psychiatric Nursing
questions.question: A client says, 'I feel like no one understands me.' Which response by the nurse demonstrates the most therapeutic communication?
choices: A. 'Don't worry, I understand you perfectly.'
B. 'Tell me more about what you're feeling.'
C. 'Everyone feels that way sometimes.'
D. 'Have you tried talking to your family?'
questions.rationale: Asking the client to elaborate encourages expression of feelings and demonstrates active listening. Option A offers false reassurance. Option C minimizes feelings. Option D gives premature advice before fully assessing the situation. Therapeutic communication focuses on the client's feelings.

questions.subject: Psychiatric Nursing
questions.question: A nurse is assessing a client who states, 'I have a plan to end my life, and I have the means to do it.' How should the nurse prioritize safety?
choices: A. Maintain confidentiality and document the statement
B. Ask the client to promise not to harm themselves
C. Implement suicide precautions and notify the treatment team immediately
D. Encourage the client to call a crisis hotline
questions.rationale: A client with a specific plan and means is at HIGH risk for suicide. Priority is safety — implement 1:1 observation, remove harmful objects, and immediately notify the treatment team. Confidentiality is overridden when safety is at risk. A 'no-harm contract' (promise) alone is insufficient for high-risk clients.

questions.subject: Community Health Nursing
questions.question: The incidence rate of dengue fever in a community is being calculated. Which data does the nurse need to compute this?
choices: A. Total number of existing dengue cases in the population
B. Number of new dengue cases during a specific time period
C. Number of dengue deaths in the community
D. Total hospital admissions for dengue
questions.rationale: Incidence rate measures NEW cases of a disease occurring in a population during a specific time period. It is calculated as: (New cases / Population at risk) × constant (e.g., 100,000). Prevalence counts ALL existing cases (old + new). Incidence helps identify risk factors and disease spread.

questions.subject: Community Health Nursing
questions.question: A nurse conducts a breast self-examination class for women in the community. This is an example of which level of prevention?
choices: A. Primary prevention
B. Secondary prevention
C. Tertiary prevention
D. Primordial prevention
questions.rationale: Secondary prevention focuses on early detection and prompt treatment of disease. BSE is a screening measure aimed at detecting breast cancer early when it is most treatable. Primary prevention prevents disease onset (e.g., vaccination). Tertiary prevents complications and rehabilitation.

questions.subject: Pharmacology
questions.question: A client is prescribed amoxicillin. Before administration, which assessment is most critical?
choices: A. Current blood pressure reading
B. History of allergies, especially to penicillin
C. Most recent kidney function tests
D. Current body temperature
questions.rationale: Amoxicillin is a penicillin-type antibiotic. Cross-reactivity between penicillins and cephalosporins can occur. Assessing for penicillin allergy is critical to prevent potentially life-threatening anaphylaxis. Always obtain a complete allergy history before administering any antibiotic.

questions.subject: Pharmacology
questions.question: A client is receiving digoxin. Which assessment finding requires the nurse to withhold the medication and notify the physician?
choices: A. Apical pulse of 62 bpm
B. Serum potassium level of 3.0 mEq/L
C. Blood pressure of 118/76 mmHg
D. Respiratory rate of 18 breaths/min
questions.rationale: Hypokalemia (K+ < 3.5 mEq/L) increases the risk of digoxin toxicity. Potassium and digoxin compete for the same receptor sites on the Na+/K+ ATPase pump. Low potassium increases digoxin binding, leading to toxicity. Signs include bradycardia, nausea, yellow-green halos. Withhold digoxin and notify physician.

questions.subject: Nutrition & Diet Therapy
questions.question: A client with chronic kidney disease (CKD) asks what dietary changes to make. Which foods should the nurse instruct the client to limit?
choices: A. Rice and pasta
B. Bananas and oranges
C. Olive oil and butter
D. White bread and crackers
questions.rationale: CKD clients need to limit potassium-rich foods because damaged kidneys cannot excrete excess potassium, leading to hyperkalemia (dangerous cardiac arrhythmias). Bananas and oranges are high in potassium. Low-potassium alternatives include apples, grapes, and berries.
